title: Typed semantic project factbase
summary: Verified project knowledge is stored as typed JSON records with provenance, evidence, relationships, lifecycle status, lexical retrieval, embeddings, and evaluation queries.
details: knowledge/records is canonical; CATALOG.json and CATALOG.md are generated views and the SQLite/embedding artifacts under knowledge/.cache are disposable.
Record kinds distinguish invariants, decisions, observed behaviors, components, incidents, questions, and history instead of flattening every statement into prose.
Status and supersession preserve what used to be true without presenting it as current consensus.
Every record carries provenance, evidence, explicit relations, retrieval aliases/questions, and a last-verification method.

tools/knowledge.py validates a schema subset, builds catalogs and FTS5, creates optional FastEmbed vectors, combines lexical and semantic ranking with reciprocal-rank fusion, expands one-hop relationships, and always injects mandatory invariants.
Evaluation cases test workflow-oriented retrieval questions rather than embedding throughput or large-scale performance.
The prototype transfers provenance/status discipline from the read-only tensor-network-cluster thesis repository without copying its expensive SLURM experiment ceremony into a cheap-test project.

provenance.note: Owner requested a workflow/schema prototype here to inform a possible later thesis-repository implementation.
Facts, reports, research questions, experiment provenance, and registry patterns informed the schema; no writes were made there.

evidence.reference: knowledge/evaluation/queries.json via tools/knowledge eval lexical and semantic
evidence.claim: Task-style queries retrieve their expected records.
The typed contract and retrieval policy are versioned with the repository.

questions: How is durable project knowledge organized?
What did the thesis repository contribute to this workflow?
Which files are canonical versus generated or disposable?

last_verified.method: Schema validation, catalog build, lexical evaluation, semantic evaluation, and representative hybrid queries.
